---
title: "Jessica Falcão"
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}
::: {.profile-left}
![](../images/equipo/jessica.jpg){.profile-photo-large}

::: {.profile-links}
[✉](mailto:jecafalcao@gmail.com)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-right}
**Postdoctoral Researcher**  
Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (INECOL)

*Interests:* [Exotic plants]{.profile-tag} [Invasion ecology]{.profile-tag} [Insect-plant interactions]{.profile-tag}

I am a biologist who focused on different areas during my formation, always fascinated by insects and their interactions with plants. This led me through biological control, ant-plant interactions, and pollination ecology to my current passion: the puzzling mechanisms of plant invasibility and invasiveness. At the Evolutionary Macroecology Lab I am studying the phylogenetic structure of exotic and native plant assemblages in Mexican montane cloud forests.

### Education

- **PhD** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (2022)
- **MSc** — Universidade Federal do Mato Grosso, Brasil (2013)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-back}
[← Back to Team](../equipo.html)
:::
